column2: In macOS, a .dmg file is a mountable {{c1::disk image}}.
column3: Finder can mount it like a drive, and Disk Utility can create and manage disk images.
column4: Do not confuse .dmg with .pkg, which runs an installer, or .app, which is an application bundle.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column9: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Cloze HighYield macOS FileTypes Source::Messer-v140

column2: In macOS, a .pkg file is an {{c1::installer package}}.
column3: Opening it runs an installation process that can place application components on the system.
column4: A .pkg runs an installer process. Some applications also include a separate uninstall program for removal.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column9: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Cloze HighYield macOS FileTypes Source::Messer-v140

column2: In macOS, an .app item is an {{c1::application bundle}}.
column3: It appears as one application in Finder while containing the files needed by that application.
column4: This bundle design is why many self-contained applications can be removed by moving the .app item to the Trash.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
